source.authority: Patterns are extracted from real Page 1 screens. Current Components page is reference-only until verified.

patterns.name: 移动端页面壳
rules: Use 390px as the default prototype width.
Keep mobile-first vertical flow.
Do not use desktop dashboard layout for MVP outputs.

patterns.name: 市场指数卡片
rules: Use finance rise/fall tokens for number and percentage.
Keep values dense and scannable.
Do not turn index cards into large marketing KPI cards.

patterns.name: 实时简报卡片
rules: Use compact row rhythm.
Keep text hierarchy clear: title, body, metadata.
Do not use long article layout unless page pattern is detail-event.

patterns.name: 快捷入口网格
rules: Use only real app-like action labels.
Do not replace missing icons with random generic icons without marking the assumption.
Keep icon and label centered in each item.

patterns.name: 底部导航
rules: Do not invent a new navigation model for page modifications.
Use active text/icon tone for the current tab.
Keep bottom navigation fixed in interactive HTML prototypes when the page scrolls.

patterns.name: 指标行
rules: Use for compact label-value financial data.
Use finance tokens for directional values.
Do not over-expand into a full card unless the data needs explanation.

patterns.name: 进度条
rules: Use semantic track and fill colors.
Progress should support rise/fall or neutral meaning depending on surrounding data.

patterns.name: AI 交易员头部
rules: Keep AI trading context financial, not generic chatbot.
Use concise explanation text and expose the primary action clearly.

patterns.name: 股票推荐卡片
rules: Use real stock-data hierarchy: name, price, performance, status.
Do not use generic product card layout.

patterns.name: 时间线事件行
rules: Use for AI trading, event details, and market timeline explanation.
Rows should stay compact and scannable.

patterns.name: 风险/状态提示模块
rules: Use for PM page-modification requests that add warnings, risk tips, or status updates.
Keep the notice visually integrated with existing card rhythm.
Do not use alert styles that feel like a web admin system.
